title: Capri Boat Rental 2026: Prices, Best Boats & Where to Set Off
html:
Renting a boat in Capri is the smartest way to explore the turquoise waters of the Tyrrhenian Sea, with prices starting from around €100 per person for a shared tour. Depart from Marina Grande, the island's main port, and set your own itinerary. BoatHire24 connects you directly with local owners so you can browse boats for your dates and compare verified listings.

Where to set off in Capri

Most boat rentals depart from Marina Grande, the ferry port connected to Naples and Sorrento. For smaller craft, Marina Piccola offers a sheltered launch point near the Faraglioni rock formations. Other departure options include the port of Cassano on the Sorrentine Peninsula and the nearby island of Ischia, a short crossing to the west. The Blue Grotto is best approached by small boat from Marina Grande. You can browse boats for your dates in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

  1. Shared motorboat tour, ideal for solo travellers or couples, visits the grottoes and Faraglioni. Capacity up to 12 guests. From €100 per person.
  2. Small motorboat (without licence), perfect for a DIY half-day, allows swimming stops. Capacity 6 guests. From €250 for four hours.
  3. Catamaran full-day charter, spacious for groups, includes a skipper and snorkel gear. Capacity up to 12 guests. From €1,200 for eight hours.
  4. Luxury motor yacht (e.g. Azimut or Fairline), crewed, with a flybridge and onboard dining. Capacity 10, 14 guests. From €2,500 for a full day.

For the Fairline model mentioned above, this motor yacht is available for weekly charters through BoatHire24.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Capri?

A private boat rental in Capri ranges from €250 for a small motorboat half-day to €3,500 for a crewed yacht full-day.

  • Shared group tour: €100, €180 per person, 2, 3 hours.
  • Self-drive motorboat (without licence): €250, €400 for half a day.
  • Catamaran with skipper: €1,200, €1,800 for a full day.
  • Crewed luxury yacht: €2,500, €3,500 for a full day, including crew and fuel.

All prices are in EUR and include standard equipment; fuel may be extra for larger craft.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Capri?

No, Italy does not require a licence for rental boats under 40hp, but your operator will confirm the exact requirements.

If you book a self-drive boat, ask the owner for a quick briefing on local no-go zones, the sea around the Blue Grotto is busy in summer and small boats must keep a 50-metre distance from the cliff.

High season runs from June to September, when the prevailing winds are the northerly Maestrale and the evening Ponente. In 2026, the shoulder months (May and October) offer calmer seas and lower prices.

At a glance

  • Price band: €100, €3,500 per trip
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 6, 14 guests
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Capri

Can I visit the Blue Grotto on a rented boat?

Yes, but only small boats (under 4 metres wide) can enter the grotto, and you must wear life jackets and avoid touching the walls.

How far is it from Capri to the Amalfi Coast by boat?

The distance from Marina Grande to Positano is roughly 14 nautical miles, a 45, 60 minute cruise in a motorboat.

Are there restaurants accessible only by boat in Capri?

Yes, several cove-side restaurants around the island, such as Da Luigi ai Faraglioni, serve lunch with mooring buoys for guests.

Whether you are planning a sunset cruise or a week-long island hop, renting a boat in Capri gives you freedom that ferries cannot match. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Capri and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
